reason: Modifying mount options in /etc/fstab for the /boot partition carries a risk of system boot failure if the syntax is incorrect or if specific bootloader components require SUID bits. While generally a standard hardening practice, any change to the boot partition configuration requires verification to ensure the system remains bootable.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential for boot failure if /etc/fstab syntax is corrupted; Risk of breaking bootloader components that may rely on SUID/SGID permissions
